title: Exact Dynamics of Topological Order Across a CDW--SPT Transition
authors: Pradip Kattel, Yicheng Tang, Natan Andrei
abstract: We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of a one-dimensional interacting system across a transition from a charge-density-wave (CDW) phase to a symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phase. Starting from a CDW initial state, we study both sudden quenches and slow ramps into the SPT regime. While the CDW order melts under both protocols, the fate of topological order is sharply different. Following a sudden quench, long-range SPT order does not emerge because the post-quench state contains a finite density of excitations above the topological ground state. In contrast, slow ramps allow the system to follow the instantaneous ground state away from the critical region, enabling the buildup of SPT order with deviations governed by Kibble-Zurek defect production. The dynamics is solvable via a unitary mapping to a quadratic fermionic Hamiltonian, allowing us to compute the Loschmidt echo, correlation functions, and string correlator. The Loschmidt rate function exhibits cusps signaling dynamical quantum phase transitions, while the correlation dynamics reveal the contrasting mechanisms governing quenches and ramps across the transition. These results demonstrate that entering the topological regime is not sufficient for the emergence of topological order; the decisive factor is the suppression of excitation production during the evolution.
summary: This paper analyzes the non-equilibrium dynamics across a CDW to SPT phase transition in 1D fermions. It demonstrates that while entering the topological regime is necessary, it is not sufficient; only slow, adiabatic evolution suppresses excitations enough to allow true long-range topological order to build up. The study uses exact fermionic mappings to track correlation functions during both sudden and gradual changes.
main_problem: The study investigates the non-equilibrium dynamics of topological order when a system transitions from a Charge-Density-Wave (CDW) phase to a Symmetry-Protected Topological (SPT) phase.
main_result: Long-range SPT order only emerges during slow, adiabatic ramps across the transition; sudden quenches fail to establish this order due to residual excitations.
method: The dynamics are solved analytically by mapping the interacting Hamiltonian to an exactly solvable quadratic fermionic form, allowing for calculations of correlation functions and the Loschmidt echo.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional interacting system (spinless fermions) modeled by a Kitaev-like Hamiltonian that exhibits CDW ($\lambda > 1$) and SPT ($-1 < \lambda < 1$) phases. The evolution is studied via quenching or ramping the coupling parameter $\lambda$.
key_observables: Loschmidt echo/rate function, CDW correlation ($\langle C^{CDW} angle$), and non-local SPT string correlator ($\langle S_{ij}(t) angle$).
important_parameters: The quench protocol (sudden vs. slow ramp), the coupling parameter $\lambda$, and the defect density ($n_q$) governed by Kibble–Zurek scaling.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on an exact unitary mapping to solve the dynamics analytically, and comparisons are made between idealized quenches and finite-time ramps.
figures_summary: Figures compare quench dynamics showing CDW melting but suppressed string order (sudden quench) versus ramp dynamics showing enhanced string order correlating with adiabaticity (slow ramp).
paper_structure: The paper compares the outcomes of two non-equilibrium protocols—a sudden quench and a slow ramp—across the CDW-SPT transition, analyzing how excitation production dictates the final topological state.
why_interesting: This work provides a rigorous theoretical framework for understanding how dynamic processes (like quenches) can fundamentally alter the realization or stability of exotic quantum phases like topological order, which is crucial for designing quantum materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Quench dynamics and finite-size scaling of CDW and SPT order. (a) Time evolution of the CDW correlator Cπ(t) after a sudden quench to λf = 0.5. The CDW order melts and saturates to a small value. Inset: the string correlator Sπ(t) remains strongly suppressed, indicating the absence of dynamically generated SPT order. (b) Finite-size scaling of the late-time Cπ(t), which vanishes as N →∞. (c) Finite-size scaling of Sπ, showing no finite intercept in the thermodynamic limit.

FIG. 2. Top panel: Ramp dynamics across the CDW–SPT transition. Slower ramps suppress CDW order and enhance string order. Inset: The overlap O(τQ) = |⟨Ωint(λf)|ψ(τQ)⟩| . approaches unity with increasing τQ, reflecting the onset of adiabatic evolution. Bottom panel: Defect density nq, ex- hibiting Kibble–Zurek scaling nq ∼τ −1/2 Q . Inset: final string order |Sπ(τQ)|, showing the approach to the adiabatic SPT state as the ramp time increases.
